practice.name: Problem 2 (2025 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
The table below shows the ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs that were used to represent different numbers. [The table of hieroglyph values and the combination of hieroglyphs are figures; see the AoPS link.] What number was represented by the given combination of hieroglyphs?

$\textbf{(A)}\ 1{,}423 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 10{,}423 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 14{,}023 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 14{,}203 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 14{,}230$

practice.name: Problem 2 (2022 AMC 8)
practice.statement:
Consider these two operations: $$a \blacklozenge b = a^2 - b^2, \qquad a \bigstar b = (a - b)^2.$$ What is $(5 \blacklozenge 3) \bigstar 6$?

$\textbf{(A)}\ -20 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 4 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 16 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 100 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 220$

practice.name: Problem 2 (2002 AMC 10A)
practice.statement:
For the nonzero numbers $a$, $b$, $c$, define $(a, b, c) = \frac{a}{b} + \frac{b}{c} + \frac{c}{a}.$ Find $(2, 12, 9).$

$\textbf{(A)}\ 4 \qquad \textbf{(B)}\ 5 \qquad \textbf{(C)}\ 6 \qquad \textbf{(D)}\ 7 \qquad \textbf{(E)}\ 8$
